articles.title: 🗞️ Araujo, Julián Álvarez, Gordon... Today’s front pages
articles.description: Today’s newspaper front pages in Spain are dominated by a full-blown earthquake in the transfer market, with FC Barcelona at the epicenter, along with a strong human and mental health angle surrounding the Blaugrana first team. Araujo’s more human side and the conflict over Julián MARCA leads with a powerful front page dedicated exclusively to Ronald Araujo . Under the headline "I had to ask for help" , the Barcelona captain admits he overcame a mental health struggle that made him "explode" in the dressing room. Now recovered, he is celebrating the league title won against Real Madrid at their stadium. Atlético de Madrid fights back AS focuses its front page directly on the club’s anger with the headline "Atleti have had enough of Barça". They describe Deco’s persistence (he met with the Argentine’s agent) as an "attitude of a small club" . Fever for "Flash Gordon" The Barcelona-based press is bursting with optimism on the transfer front. Both newspapers confirm that English winger Anthony Gordon has already landed in Barcelona, passed his medical, and has the full approval of Hansi Flick . Mundo Deportivo headlines "Flash Gordon and now Julián" , stressing that Deco never stops and is looking to seal the Argentine signing before the World Cup. SPORT headlines "Gordon is already here" and adds that after the Englishman, the Barça board has made its first offer for Julián and has restarted its all-out push to bring in Bernardo Silva as well. This article was translated into English by Artificial Intelligence. You can read the original version in 🇪🇸 here.
